text: Endurance performance can be maintained for up to 15 weeks when training frequency is reduced to as few as 2 sessions per week, provided exercise intensity is maintained.
Endurance performance can be maintained for up to 15 weeks when exercise volume is reduced by 33-66% (as low as 13-26 minutes per session), provided exercise intensity is maintained.
Exercise intensity (exercising heart rate for endurance, relative load for strength) is the key variable for maintaining physical performance, more important than frequency or volume.

Strength and muscle size in younger populations can be maintained for up to 32 weeks with as little as 1 strength training session per week and 1 set per exercise, provided relative load is maintained.
specific_value: 1 session/week, 1 set/exercise for up to 32 weeks

text: Older populations may require at least 2 strength training sessions per week and 2-3 sets per exercise to maintain muscle size, while maintaining exercise intensity.
Maintaining exercising heart rate at pre-reduction levels is critical when reducing endurance training frequency or volume to preserve performance.
